attacking_style: Attacks from breakaways on final climbs (4-8km to go), then sustains power through technical descents and flat runs to finish. Specializes in long-range solo efforts rather than explosive accelerations, particularly effective on steep gradients (20%+) where diesel power matters.
team_tactics: Functions as satellite rider in breaks while team controls behind, or infiltrates breaks when close on GC to force rival teams' hands. Limited domestique support in attacks—relies on independent climbing/TT ability rather than lead-outs.
terrain_preference: Steep gradient finishes (20-23%) and technical parcours combining climb-descent-flat sequences. Strong time-trialist on punchy technical courses. Vulnerable on sustained moderate gradients where pure climbers can apply consistent pressure.
vulnerability: Poor tactical decision-making in small group situations—refuses cooperation, attacks when sprinting would win, shows impatience. Fades in final sprints despite making winning moves. Cannot sustain explosive early efforts through to finish.
key_patterns: Wins Grand Tour stages from breakaways by attacking final climb, won Giro/Tour/Vuelta stages 2022-2023
Attacks 4-8km from finish on climbs, then time-trials solo using descent/flat power (6 observations)
Tactical impatience in small groups costs results—refuses cooperation, crashes attacking alone unnecessarily (3 instances)
Functions as GC satellite rider, infiltrates breaks when close on GC to pressure rivals (Catalunya 2021, Giro 2022)
Strong early efforts but cannot sustain—11-second advantage fades, caught in final km multiple times
form_trajectory: neutral: Completed Grand Tour stage win treble in 2023 but showed tactical errors and form inconsistency (crashed attacking alone, faded in sprints, absent from expected finales). Strong peaks but unreliable execution.
